famous quote:
"In our world," said Eustace, "a star is a huge ball of flaming gas."
Even in your world, my son, that is not what a star is, but only what it is made of."
-- C.S. Lewis (The Voyage of the Dawn Treader (Chronicles of Narnia, #3))
Interview Text:
Bryan Farrar:

(part 1)

This is a story I recorded during an interview with Bryan Farrar: 

There was a time when I was working with Youth with a Mission (living off the generosity of whoever would support me). I was living in Maui which was expensive, and traveling to Asia to work with our long term partners over there. My roommate Ben was doing a six week trip to check in on all of our ministry partners in Asia. One day he said, "Hey Brian, why don't you come with me?!" I was 25 and broke, with no thought of how to pay for this but I said, yeah I'm in! 

My goal on this trip was to get video for marketing purposes. I started looking for airline flights. I imagined it would be something like $800 from Atlanta to Bangkok (We had some ministry partners to get me the rest of the way if I could just get to them in Bancock).

I started looking and it was like $3500! I started tracking prices. Everything was hovering around 3000 to 3500. I asked my other roommate Fritz' advice. He had lived in China before and flown to Thailand for Tsunami relief and stuff. He owned a motorcycle rental business in Maui. He said, "Have you prayed about it? Just pray how much he wants you to spend on this flight and if he wants you to go he'll tell you how much he wants you to spend." They prayed together. And decided to wait a couple days. I told Fritz that the number 1200 kept coming up. Fritz told me "Okay, don't spend more than 1200 on this flight." 
I found one flight on Korean air that was $1350. I thought for sure I should get it; when am I going to find it this cheap again. Fritz said, "Don't you buy that flight! It's not 1200!" 2 weeks later another one came up $1189 so I bought it on a credit card because I still didn't have money to pay for it. One week before I needed to pay the credit card (I had always lived frugally and never had to pay a credit card before) I get an email from a friend's mom back in Georgia. She used to follow my blog. She said, "Hey Bryan, I've been meaning to send you a check. But I haven't gotten to it." I received the check and it was $1200. The note said, "I was meaning to give you 100 per month for the past year. 

It was not a fun flight (atlanta to chicago, to tokyo, to bangcok.) I had to hang out in the Tokyo airport for 6 hours. I had to get some lunch. I bought some Teriaki chicken (why not? I was in Japan!). But I found out later that with the exchange rate, it came out to 11$. With my flight being $1,189, and then adding this $11 of food, this equals the 1,200 God had put in my mind! 
The common theme was, say yes to good things, but make sure you include God in that conversation. A lot of times he will speak into it. If there's something specific to be done, He'll tell you. Also, have the patience to stick with what he told you. Not 1350. He said 1200!
Music Video Instructions: Imagine God telling you this is how he loves you. The predictibility of maps, is not what God offers. He offers relationship.
